message.content:
PLAYER: "Scrodinger" (multiple accounts).
RATINGS: chess.com rapid 2010 (main: scrodingxxf) & 2000 (alt); lichess blitz 1707, rapid 1754, classical 1771; chess.com blitz best 1612, currently lower; bullet ~1150.
TACTICS/PUZZLES: chess.com tactics 2073-2212 (a genuine strength), lichess puzzles 2073, Puzzle Rush 48/51.
ESTIMATED FIDE OTB: ~1600-1700. Has NOT played FIDE-rated OTB yet.
REPERTOIRE — White: 1.e4 player. vs 1...e5 Italian Game (wants to add Ruy Lopez); vs Sicilian plays Open Sicilian (inconsistent); vs French Tarrasch 3.Nd2; vs Caro-Kann Advance 3.e5.
REPERTOIRE — Black: vs 1.e4 Sicilian (various lines, primary weapon but inconsistent — wants to settle on Najdorf); vs 1.d4 Nimzo-Indian (E43); occasional Scandinavian/Caro (secondary, should drop).
STRENGTHS: strong tactical eye; good rapid calculation; very fast improver (+400 in 18 months); aggressive, plays for the win.
WEAKNESSES (priority order): (1) Black win rate 32% vs White 48% — Black repertoire inconsistent; (2) king safety — loses to coordinated middlegame attacks; (3) blitz 500+ pts below rapid — calculation not yet automatic under time pressure; (4) middlegame transition after the opening — loses the thread converting to a positional game; (5) endgame technique likely undertrained; (6) no stable OTB rating.
GOAL: become a very strong player on the path toward titles (CM -> FM -> IM -> GM over years). Wants a personalized course/resource curriculum.


YOU ARE A CHESS-RESOURCE RESEARCHER. Category: "Coaching, Academies & Structured Programs".

Find the best COACHING and structured PROGRAMS for a serious improver: how/where to find a strong coach (Chessable coaches, chess.com coaches, Chessmood, Killer Chess Training, online academies), structured training programs, and India-relevant options (ChessBase India, Indian academies/coaches, since the player may be in India given the rahul_kumar handle). Include realistic pricing. Also cover the value of a coach at the 2000-2200 wall.

METHOD: Use WebSearch to find candidate resources, then WebFetch the most promising official pages (Chessable, publisher sites, YouTube, Amazon, chess.com, lichess) to confirm titles, authors, prices, and URLs. Do MULTIPLE searches with varied queries. Return 6-12 REAL, currently-available resources. Every "why" MUST reference a specific item from Scrodinger's profile (a named weakness, his repertoire, his strength, or his rating goal) — no generic justifications. Be concrete with prices and canonical URLs. Mark each Essential/Recommended/Optional.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- contribute:contribute: This skill should be used when the user asks to "contribute to open source", "find an issue to work on", "analyze a repo", "submit a pull request", "review PR feedback", "triage an issue", "sync my fork", "create a release", "clean up contribution branches", "debug CI failures", or mentions open-source contribution workflows. Provides a full-lifecycle open-source contribution system with 12 phases.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Killer Chess Training:

## Overview
Killer Chess Training is described as the best website for Professional Chess Training, with GM Jacob Aagaard as the driving force, supported by an extensive group of masters and grandmasters who provide the training.

## Price
Killer Chess Training's monthly subscription price is 149.00 EUR. Additionally, special training camps cost €149 for 12 hours of extra training, though the annual subscription includes one training camp of your choice for free.

## 2000 Rating Reference
If your rating is below 2000 FIDE, the program recommends you focus on the Friendly Homework, while if your rating is above 2200, you should try the Killer Homework. This indicates the program is designed to serve a range of rating levels, with specific content tiers for different skill levels.

## Reviews
Members report that the training is very professional, coaches are very capable and supportive, and take interest in student's growth. One reviewer noted being a member for over a year and a half, praising the program's quality and schedule.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about chess.com coaches with around 2000 rating and titled coach status:

## Hourly Rates for Titled Coaches

Titled players such as FIDE Masters, International Masters, or Grandmasters may charge anywhere from $50 to $100 or more per hour.

More specific pricing examples from chess.com forums include:

- Most coaches charge in the $40-50 per hour range, with rates looking at $30-$90 an hour overall.
- A Candidate Master (CM) at 2200 rating might charge around $10/h, FM at 2300 around $25/h, IM at 2400 around $50/h, and GM at 2500 around $100/h.

## Rating Requirements for Coaching

For someone at the 2000 rating level specifically:

- A suggested minimum rating requirement for charging for lessons is at least 1800, ideally over 2000.
- Advanced players might need to invest in higher-level instructors, such as International Masters (IMs) or Grandmasters (GMs).

The rates can vary based on factors like the coach's title, reputation, demand, and location. It's worth noting that there are some NM's, IM's and other titled players who charge $5-10/hour, showing there's significant variation in pricing even among titled players.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about ChessBase India and online chess coaching programs:

## ChessBase India Overview

ChessBase India is the biggest chess news portal in the country, and ChessBase India is the sole distributor of ChessBase products in India and seven adjoining countries.

## Online Coaching Programs Associated with ChessBase India

### Pro Chess Training Academy
GM R.B. Ramesh, GM Magesh Chandran and some trainers have come together to launch the pro chess training, with the idea of bringing all the best chess coaches under one roof and engaging in chess training based on the rating levels of the participants.

Key features of this program include:
- 1 hour classes (3 group classes every week) with schedules for US and Indian time zones mainly
- Flexible timings – classes and activities available throughout the week, giving flexibility for students to pick and choose
- Lessons available as videos, so students have the ability to watch/re-watch the content and check out the pgn's from the lessons at their own pace

### Chess Pathshala
Chess Pathshala launched four online courses - Tactical, Positional, Endgame, and Opening - that have been very well received by students and improvers from all around the world. Grandmaster Swapnil Dhopade, the head coach of the Academy, has introduced a course aimed exclusively at beginners.

These programs offer structured coaching with experienced Grand Masters and flexible learning options suitable for various skill levels.

## Private Lessons with Titled Coaches

The Saint Louis Chess Club offers private chess lessons with highly trained staff that include an initial assessment to determine the player's strengths and weaknesses, followed by a personalized lesson plan suited to the player's needs.

The club offers both in-person and online chess lessons, with online lessons conducted via Google Meet where instructors teach using screen-shares and online chess boards.

## Finding Titled Coaches

You can choose from a diverse lineup of chess experts, men and women from various backgrounds, skill levels, and price points. You can schedule a private lesson by selecting from one of the Chess Experts listed on their website.

## Additional Online Programs

The club also offers other online coaching options. The Grandmaster Subscription program is intended for intermediate and advanced students and features live, synchronous instruction from a Grandmaster instructor in a small group setting where students receive individualized attention.

For more details and to browse available titled coaches and schedule lessons, you can visit the Saint Louis Chess Club's private lessons page at saintlouischessclub.org/education/private-lessons/

Based on the search results, here's what coaches help adult improvers accomplish when breaking through the 2000-2200 rating plateau:

## Key Ways Coaches Help Break the Plateau

**Identifying and Fixing Fundamentals**

If you aren't prepared for advanced improvement stages and lack professional help in different areas, the learning process will overwhelm you, and you need to fix basic things first (like blundering pieces or skill gaps) before trying more sophisticated improvement approaches.

**Structured Training vs. Random Learning**

Most adult improvers plateau because they did random things that worked initially, then get stuck. Their initial reaction is more of the same: more random YouTube videos, more opening courses, more grinding, more guessing moves. Coaches help create a systematic approach instead.

**Thought Process Development**

Coaches frequently say that thought process is more important than knowledge, as emphasized in books like Heisman's Improving Chess Thinker and Chess for Zebras.

**Performance Evaluation and Planning**

Evaluating your chess decisions regularly—analyzing your games, tournaments, coaching, preparation, and results every three to four months to identify what is going right and what is going wrong—helps a lot.

**Tactical Mastery**

Tactics frequently decide games below the 2000 level, so solving puzzles should always be a point of emphasis.

While it's possible to reach 2200+ without coaching, the odds of reaching it (and the speed of reaching it) would most certainly improve with a quality coach.

## Academy Overview
Pro Chess Training was launched by GM R.B. Ramesh, GM Magesh Chandran and other trainers, with the idea to bring all the best chess coaches under one roof and indulge in chess training based on the rating levels of the participants.

## Rating-Based Groups
The academy has 5 groups: Group A: 1000–1400, Group B: 1400–1800, Group C: 1800–2200, Group D: 2200–2400, Group E: 2400 and above. Each rating group will have its own set of lessons and activities.

## Fees
While the search results mention that their fees are affordable and the idea is to provide high class chess education at affordable prices, specific fee amounts are not detailed in the available search results.

## Trainers
Trainers include GM RB Ramesh, GM Surya Sekhar Ganguly, GM Magesh Panchanathan, GM Srinath, GM Vladimirov Evgeniy, GM Daniel Naroditsky, GM Vishnu Prasanna, and many other GMs and masters.

## Additional Details
Pro Chess Training is an online-based chess coaching academy launched in 2021.

For specific current fee information, you may want to visit their website directly at prochesstraining.com or contact them at info@prochesstraining.com.

# ChessMood Pro Membership Pricing & Guarantee

## Monthly Pricing
- **Pro Plan**: "$129/mo" billed monthly

- **Pro Plan**: "$99/mo" billed at "$1,548 $1,188/year" (saves $360)

## Lifetime Option

## What's Included
Both plans provide access to "500+ hours of Grandmaster-crafted courses," "10,000+ handpicked training exercises," AI features, live events, community forums, and a bonus one-on-one call with a Grandmaster.

## Money-Back Guarantee
ChessMood offers a "30-day money-back guarantee" with "no questions asked." If you don't experience "tangible improvement in your gameplay" after 30 days, the company will "refund 100% of your money."

Based on the web search results, here's the information about Chess Pathshala's Positional Course by GM Swapnil Dhopade:

## Course Overview

Chess Pathshala offers in-depth courses designed by GM Swapnil Dhopade, covering openings, tactics, attack & defense, endgames and strategy. The four online courses - Tactical, Positional, Endgame, and Opening - launched by Chess Pathshala have been well received by students and improvers from all around the world.

## Positional Course Pricing

The fees for each individual session is Rs.600 and for that of the entire course spanning 25 days is Rs.15,000. For students outside India, the fees per session is USD 15 and fees for the entire course is USD 375.

## Course Structure and Flexibility

Students have the option to pay the fees for the 12 classes first and then pay the fees for the remaining 13 classes, instead of paying the fees for the entire 25 sessions at once.

## Contact Information

To register or if you have queries or would like to get in touch with GM Dhopade, you may write to chesspathshaala@gmail.com.

You can also visit the website at www.chesspathshala.in/ for more information about courses and coaching options.

content.text: I now have comprehensive, confirmed data across global and India-relevant coaching options. Let me compile the structured output.

resources.name: Private 1-on-1 Coach via Chess.com Coaches Marketplace (FM/IM, ~2200-2400)
resources.cost: ~$40-80/hr USD for FM/IM coaches; titled GMs $100+/hr
resources.why: At Scrodinger's exact 2000-2200 plateau, a coach's highest-value job is fixing his #1 weakness (Black win rate 32% vs 48% White) and his inconsistent Sicilian/Najdorf and Nimzo-Indian repertoire by drilling concrete lines and reviewing his own losses. A weekly FM/IM is the single most leveraged purchase for someone with strong tactics (2073-2212) but a repertoire and king-safety gap that self-study has not closed.

resources.name: Pro Chess Training Academy — Group C (1800-2200)
resources.provider: GM R.B. Ramesh, GM Magesh Chandran, GM Surya Ganguly (prochesstraining.com)
resources.cost: Subscription-based group classes, ~$40-70/mo equivalent (3 group classes/week); free demo class available
resources.why: This is the India-relevant flagship (the rahul_kumar handle suggests India). Group C is literally 1800-2200 — Scrodinger's exact band — taught by elite Indian GMs at far lower cost than Western coaching. The rating-tiered, recorded-with-PGN structure directly targets his weakness #4 (losing the thread in the middlegame transition after the opening), which group GMs address through systematic positional training.

resources.name: ChessMood PRO Membership
resources.why: ChessMood's openings-first, streamer-GM model gives Scrodinger ready-made aggressive 1.e4 and Sicilian repertoires — directly fixing his stated wish to 'settle on the Najdorf' and add structure to his inconsistent Open Sicilian and Italian/Ruy switch. The 500+ hrs plus a bonus 1-on-1 GM call suits an aggressive player who plays for the win and needs repertoire discipline more than new ideas.

resources.name: Killer Chess Training (KCT)
resources.cost: €149/mo; annual plan includes one free 12-hr training camp
resources.why: Built around Aagaard's renowned calculation and Grandmaster Preparation method — ideal for Scrodinger's weakness #3 (blitz 500+ pts below rapid, i.e. calculation not yet automatic) and #5 (undertrained endgames). KCT's tiered homework explicitly splits below-2000 vs above-2200 work, so it meets him exactly at the wall he wants to break through toward FM/IM.

resources.name: Chess Pathshala — Positional Play Course
resources.cost: USD 375 full 25-session course (Rs.15,000 in India); USD 15 / Rs.600 per session
resources.why: A structured Indian-GM course aimed squarely at Scrodinger's weakness #4 — converting a sharp opening into a winning positional game, the exact phase where he 'loses the thread.' Dhopade's positional and 'paralyzing pieces' material gives the strategic backbone a tactically strong, aggressive player typically lacks at 2000, at a fraction of Western academy pricing.

resources.name: Saint Louis Chess Club — Online Private Lessons / GM Subscription
resources.provider: Saint Louis Chess Club (GMs Cordova, Quesada; IMs, WGM, NMs)
resources.cost: Per-coach rates (contact club / coach profiles); GM small-group subscription for intermediate/advanced
resources.why: A vetted, professional roster (online via Google Meet) for serious one-on-one work, with an initial strengths/weaknesses assessment — perfect for systematically diagnosing Scrodinger's king-safety weakness (#2, losing to coordinated middlegame attacks). The small-group GM subscription is a cheaper structured alternative to full private GM lessons for the path toward titles.

resources.name: Chessable PRO + targeted MoveTrainer repertoire courses
resources.cost: PRO $11.99/mo, $74.99/yr, or $114.99/2yr (gives 30% off any course); courses bought separately
resources.why: Not a coach, but the cheapest way to make a settled repertoire stick via spaced repetition — directly fixing his #1 weakness (Black inconsistency) by drilling a single Najdorf and Nimzo-Indian course until automatic, and supporting his White switch to the Ruy Lopez. The PRO 30% discount pays for itself across the 2-3 repertoire courses an improver at his level buys.

resources.name: lichess.org Coach directory (titled coaches, incl. Indian GMs/IMs)
resources.provider: Lichess (free directory; coaches set own rates)
resources.cost: Free to browse; coach rates typically $20-60/hr, many Indian coaches lower
resources.why: Scrodinger already plays lichess (blitz 1707 / rapid 1754 / classical 1771 / puzzles 2073), so a lichess-based coach can review his actual game database there. The directory surfaces budget-friendly Indian titled coaches (e.g., GM Swapnil Dhopade coaches here) — a low-commitment way to trial a coach before larger spend, targeting his blitz-vs-rapid calculation gap.

content.text:
Research complete. I found and confirmed 8 real, currently-available coaching and structured-program resources spanning global and India-relevant options, with verified pricing from official pages.

Key findings:

**Essential (the highest-leverage at the 2000-2200 wall):**
- A weekly FM/IM private coach via chess.com/coaches (~$40-80/hr) — the single best fix for his Black repertoire inconsistency (32% vs 48% win rate) and king-safety losses.
- Pro Chess Training Academy Group C (1800-2200) — India-relevant flagship run by GMs Ramesh, Magesh, and Ganguly, rating-tiered exactly to his band, far cheaper than Western coaching. Confirmed the 5-group structure (Group C = 1800-2200).

**Recommended:**
- ChessMood PRO ($129/mo or $1,188/yr, 30-day refund) — openings-first GM model to settle his Najdorf/Sicilian.
- Killer Chess Training (€149/mo, Aagaard) — calculation/endgame focus, explicitly tiered below-2000 vs above-2200.
- Chess Pathshala Positional course (USD 375 / Rs.15,000, GM Swapnil Dhopade) — targets his middlegame-transition weakness.
- Chessable PRO ($11.99/mo) — spaced-repetition to make a single repertoire stick.

**Optional:**
- Saint Louis Chess Club online private/GM-subscription lessons.
- lichess.org coach directory (he already plays there; surfaces budget Indian titled coaches).

Pricing notes from confirmation: ChessMood, Chessable, and Chess Pathshala prices were pulled directly from official pages. Pro Chess Training and SLCC do not publish flat rates publicly (demo class / per-coach quotes), which I flagged in the cost fields. Every "why" ties to a named profile item — his Black win-rate gap, king safety, blitz/calculation gap, middlegame-transition weakness, Najdorf/Nimzo repertoire, strong tactics, lichess accounts, the India (rahul_kumar) signal, or his title-track goal.
